title: Status filter does not accept "dead" and "restarting" in list containers podman API
body:
### Issue Description

In [Podman API docs of "List containers"](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html?version=latest#tag/containers/operation/ContainerListLibpod):

> filters: string, A JSON encoded value of the filters (a map[string][]string) to process on the containers list. Available filters:
>
> ...
>
> status=(created, restarting, running, removing, paused, exited or dead)

However, `/libpod/containers/json` endpoint does not accept "dead" and "restarting".

### Steps to reproduce the issue

`curl --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/v5/libpod/containers/json?filters=%7B%22status%22%3A%5B%22dead%22%5D%7D'`

`curl --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/v5/libpod/containers/json?filters=%7B%22status%22%3A%5B%22restarting%22%5D%7D'`

### Describe the results you received

```
{"cause":"invalid argument","message":"unknown container state: dead: invalid argument","response":500}
```

```
{"cause":"invalid argument","message":"unknown container state: restarting: invalid argument","response":500}
```

### Describe the results you expected

A JSON list like this:

```console
> curl --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock 'http://d/v5/libpod/containers/json?filters=%7B%22status%22%3A%5B%22running%22%5D%7D'
[{"AutoRemove":true,"Command":["/usr/bin/fish"], ...]
```

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

`podman.socket` in user systemd started.

### Additional information

This breaks some programs using Docker API, as Docker supports `dead` state.
